diagnosis: Melanoma Stage IA (post-excision) — surveillance dermoscopy showing benign nevus, no recurrence
evidence_summary: 29-year-old female with history of Stage IA melanoma (Breslow 0.6mm), status post wide local excision in June 2025. Presenting for 3-month surveillance dermoscopy. New pigmented lesion on left forearm examined: dermoscopy shows regular pigment network, symmetric structure, no regression or blue-white veil — classified as benign compound nevus. No concerning features for melanoma recurrence. Surgical scar well-healed.
timeline_impact: Post-melanoma surveillance at 3-month interval as per NCCN guidelines. June 2025: Excisional biopsy confirmed melanoma Stage IA (Breslow 0.6mm, no ulceration, mitotic rate 0/mm²). Wide local excision July 2025 with clear margins. September 2025: First surveillance — no recurrence, new benign lesion identified and monitored. Continue 3-monthly dermoscopy for 2 years, then 6-monthly for next 3 years.
plan: Photograph and map the new benign nevus on left forearm for future comparison
Patient education: monthly self-skin examination, ABCDE criteria review
Sun protection counseling: SPF 50+, protective clothing, avoid peak UV hours
Annual lymph node examination (no sentinel lymph node biopsy required for Stage IA <0.8mm)
No systemic therapy indicated for Stage IA melanoma

findings.explanation: Dermoscopy shows symmetric globular pattern, regular pigment network, no regression structures, blue-white veil, or atypical vascular features. Consistent with benign compound nevus.
Excision scar well-healed, no satellite lesions, no in-transit metastases. Full-body survey negative for suspicious lesions.

reasoning_trace.thought: Patient has a history of Stage IA melanoma — 3-month surveillance is standard of care.
New pigmented lesion requires careful dermoscopic evaluation to rule out new primary melanoma or metastasis.
Dermoscopy features (regular network, symmetry, no blue-white veil) are reassuring — benign compound nevus.
5-year survival for Stage IA melanoma is ~99% — low risk but continued surveillance is essential.

findings.explanation: Regular pigment network, symmetric globular pattern.

relevant_records.summary: Dermoscopy: irregularly pigmented lesion, high suspicion — biopsy recommended
relevant_records.clinical_relevance: Original lesion that was biopsied and confirmed as melanoma.
Confirmed melanoma diagnosis — establishes surveillance need.

relevant_records.summary: Post-wide local excision — clear margins, healing well
relevant_records.clinical_relevance: Surgical treatment completed with clear margins.

history_search.timeline_context: Stage IA melanoma patient at 3-month post-excision surveillance. All prior treatments completed with clear margins. Currently in the high-frequency surveillance phase (Q3M for 2 years).

judge_verdict.reasoning: Image analysis and history search both support a benign finding with no evidence of melanoma recurrence. Dermoscopic features are classic for compound nevus.

doctor_notes: Benign lesion confirmed on dermoscopy. Patient reassured. Continuing Q3M surveillance per protocol.
